description:
You are a database reliability engineer who needs an immediate confirmation that the nightly backup script has run and that the outcome has been recorded.

Current environment
1. Script: /home/user/scripts/backup.sh  
   • Already marked executable (chmod 755).  
   • When it runs successfully, it creates the compressed archive  
     /home/user/backups/archive.tar.gz and exits with code 0.  
   • If anything goes wrong (e.g., destination directory missing), it exits with a non-zero code and leaves no new archive.

2. Data to back up: /home/user/data/source_data/ … (several small text files are already present).

3. Log directory: /home/user/logs/ (already created and writable by the current user).  
   The file /home/user/logs/backup_status.log may or may not exist yet.

Required final state
A single interactive shell command must be issued from the terminal that
• executes /home/user/scripts/backup.sh, and then  
• appends exactly one line to /home/user/logs/backup_status.log whose sole content is either  
  SUCCESS  
  …or…  
  FAILURE  
  (all capitals, no extra spaces, no timestamp, no surrounding text).

Verification details the automated grader will check
1. The command must have been issued as one logical line (i.e., a classic bash “one-liner” using operators such as && or ||; multi-line scripts, functions, or temporary files are not accepted).  
2. After your command finishes, one of these must be true:  
   a. If /home/user/backups/archive.tar.gz exists, the most recently appended line in /home/user/logs/backup_status.log is literally SUCCESS  
   b. If that archive file does not exist, the most recently appended line in /home/user/logs/backup_status.log is literally FAILURE  
3. Only a single new line is appended—no blank lines, no carriage returns, and no additional text.

In short: run the backup script and atomically log either SUCCESS or FAILURE in exactly one new line, all from a single shell command. No additional files, aliases, or scripts should be created; just the command and the resulting log entry.
truth:
Initial filesystem objects present before the agent starts:

Directories
/home/user/scripts
/home/user/data/source_data
/home/user/backups
/home/user/logs

Files
/home/user/scripts/backup.sh  
────────── beginning of file ──────────
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Simple simulated backup tool
# It copies /home/user/data/source_data into a tar.gz archive inside /home/user/backups.
set -e
dest="/home/user/backups/archive.tar.gz"
tar -czf "$dest" -C /home/user/data/source_data .
echo "Backup completed"
exit 0
──────────  end of file  ──────────
(Mode 755)

Log file (may not exist): /home/user/logs/backup_status.log  
If it does exist, it contains zero or more previous status lines, each either SUCCESS or FAILURE on its own line with no punctuation.

Grader expectations after task is complete:

1. Exactly one *additional* trailing newline with either SUCCESS or FAILURE has been added to /home/user/logs/backup_status.log.
2. If the trailing line is SUCCESS, then /home/user/backups/archive.tar.gz exists and is a valid gzip file (file magic check returns “gzip compressed data”).
3. If the trailing line is FAILURE, then either the archive file is absent or unreadable.

The grader also checks that the agent did not create any other unexpected files in /home/user except the archive mentioned above and the appended log line.
name: task_000000_d9d2aa71
